qa_pairs.question: Why are some software packages installed only at specific sites instead of being generally available through CVMFS?
qa_pairs.answer: Certain software packages are not installed in CVMFS, a distributed file system, but are installed locally at particular sites. This is typically due to license restrictions on those specific packages.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of Galaxy software, and how can a group get an instance of it on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Galaxy is a scientific workflow, data integration, and data analysis platform designed to make computational biology accessible to research scientists without extensive programming or systems administration experience. Any group on the Cedar cluster can have one Galaxy instance, which will run under a shared account created by administrators. To set up Galaxy, users must contact technical support.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to use the SAS software suite on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The SAS software suite on the Cedar cluster is licensed software belonging to users from the Alberta School of Business, who are eligible to run it.
